The Scenario

When an Inspection Is Announced,
Everyone Has Questions

A Typical Clinical Trial Lifecycle Moment

  • A medical device or pharma company is running multiple pivotal trials across therapeutic areas — each generating hundreds of clinical documents in an eTMF system
  • A regulatory agency announces a pre-approval inspection — the organization has weeks, not months, to demonstrate audit readiness across every ICH-GCP TMF zone
  • Protocols have been amended multiple times; informed consent forms vary by site — version control is fragmented across document repositories
  • Leadership needs a competitive landscape briefing for the board — but trial registry data is scattered and stale by the time it reaches a slide deck
  • Every persona in the organization has an urgent, unanswered question — and today, answering any of them takes days of manual effort
Four Personas, Four Questions

CMO / VP Clinical Operations

“Which trials have the lowest document completeness — and where is our risk exposure highest?”

TMF Manager / Document Controller

“Are all sites on the current protocol version? Are any consent forms outdated?”

VP Regulatory Affairs

“Can I get an audit readiness report — completeness by TMF zone, gaps, and action items?”

Head of Clinical Strategy

“What are competitors doing in our therapeutic area? How do their trial designs compare?”

Today, answering any of these takes days. What if a single AI-powered platform could answer all four in seconds?

The Imperative

Why Now?
The Industry-Wide TMF Governance Gap

Two pain points are converging across the industry — and manual processes cannot keep pace with growing trial portfolios.

TMF Audit Readiness Takes Days

  • Inspection announced — TMF teams manually pull document inventories trial by trial, zone by zone from the eTMF system
  • Version control gaps — protocol amendments and informed consent forms drift across sites, often discovered only during internal audits
  • No cross-trial visibility — governance boards rely on manual spreadsheet consolidation to track completeness across studies
  • Reactive, not proactive — gaps are found during inspections instead of being prevented ahead of time

Competitive Intelligence Is Manual

  • Board meeting prep — strategy teams manually search ClinicalTrials.gov and copy results into slide decks
  • Data is stale by the time it reaches leadership — competitor trials change status weekly
  • No cross-reference between internal protocol designs and competitor trial registrations
  • Multiple concurrent trials — monitoring competitive landscapes manually across therapeutic areas is unsustainable

“What if the platform you already own could turn days of manual effort into seconds of AI-powered insight?”

The Architecture

End-to-End on Snowflake

From Veeva Vault to governed AI console — every layer runs inside your Snowflake account. The data never leaves your security perimeter.

TMF Governance Console

App Walkthrough • Screen 1
Trial Portfolio Overview
Persona: CMO / VP Clinical Operations
1
Completeness Scoring

Each trial card shows real-time document completeness — ALLIANCE at 83%, TRISCEND II at 75%. Gaps are immediately visible without opening a single spreadsheet.

Cortex Analyst
2
RAG Status Badges

Red/Amber/Green badges per trial based on document age, completeness thresholds, and IRB expiry dates. The CMO sees risk at a glance — no meeting required.

Semantic View Metrics
3
Document Freshness

Average days since last document upload per trial. ALT-FLOW II has only 2 documents — expected for an early-stage program. ALLIANCE needs attention before FDA inspection.

Real-Time Analytics
Snowflake Features Powering This Screen

Semantic View

TMF_ANALYTICS_VIEW defines 7 metrics and 14 dimensions over DOCUMENT_METADATA

Flask + Snowflake Connector

Flask API queries DOCUMENT_METADATA via snowflake-connector-python, computes completeness per trial

SPCS Service

React frontend renders trial cards with Tailwind CSS — served from Snowflake compute

App Walkthrough • Screen 2
Trial Drilldown — Document Inventory
Persona: TMF Manager / Document Controller
1
Document Inventory

Click into ALLIANCE and see every document — protocol versions (v3.0, v4.0, v5.0), ICFs by site (Site-001 Cleveland Clinic, Site-002 Mayo), regulatory submissions.

DOCUMENT_METADATA Table
2
Version Tracking

Amendment history is tracked automatically via AI_EXTRACT. See that Site-001 has ICF v3.1 (current) while checking whether any site is using an outdated consent form.

AI_EXTRACT + Version Control
3
Completeness Status

Each document shows its current/outdated status with a RAG badge. Summary cards at the top show total documents, current count, outdated count, and average document age for the selected trial.

RAG Status + Summary Metrics
Snowflake Features Powering This Screen

AI_EXTRACT

Structured metadata extraction: trial name, doc type, version, PI, site ID, IRB expiry, amendment number

AI_PARSE_DOCUMENT

OCR-mode full-text extraction from clinical PDFs into searchable markdown text

Normalized Metadata

21 document records with 12 structured fields — queryable, filterable, governable

Cortex Search + eTMF Document Analysis
App Walkthrough • Screen 3
AI Agent — The Unifier
Persona: All Personas — One Agent, Four Tools
1
Cortex Search

Semantic search across 21 parsed clinical documents. Ask “What changed between ALLIANCE protocol v4 and v5?” and get grounded answers with source citations.

etmf_search Tool
2
Cortex Analyst

Text-to-SQL over the Semantic View. Ask “Which sites have overdue uploads for ENCIRCLE?” and get SQL results rendered as inline tables.

tmf_analytics Tool
3
Audit Report Gen

Custom stored procedure that queries metadata, searches key documents, and calls CORTEX.COMPLETE to generate formatted audit readiness reports.

audit_report_gen Tool
How the Agent Orchestrates

Auto-Routing

Agent analyzes the question and selects the right tool automatically — no user intervention needed

Multi-Tool Chains

Complex questions trigger multiple tools in sequence — search + analyst + report generation

SPCS Security

DATA_AGENT_RUN executes inside your Snowflake account — data never leaves the security perimeter

One-Click Audit Reports
Persona: VP Regulatory Affairs / Quality
1
Executive Summary

One-click “Generate Audit Report” per trial produces a comprehensive executive summary with overall completeness score and RAG status assessment.

CORTEX.COMPLETE + LLM
2
Zone-by-Zone Analysis

ICH-GCP mandated TMF zones analyzed individually — Zone 1 Trial Management, Zone 5 Regulatory, Zone 8 Third Parties. Specific gaps identified per zone.

TMF Governance Framework
3
Risk Items & Actions

Prioritized action items: missing documents, upcoming IRB expirations, amendment discrepancies. The report your TMF Manager would spend 3 days building — generated on demand.

Automated Risk Scoring
Snowflake Features Powering This Screen

GENERATE_AUDIT_REPORT

Python stored procedure queries metadata, searches key docs, calls COMPLETE for formatted report

CORTEX.COMPLETE

llama3.1-70b generates structured markdown sections: summary, inventory, gaps, recommendations

Agent Custom Tool

Generic tool type connects the stored procedure to the Cortex Agent for conversational access

Competitive Intelligence — ClinicalTrials.gov

Competitor Landscape

clinical_trials_registry Tool
2
Internal + External

Cross-reference Edwards internal protocol designs with competitor trial registrations. Compare ENCIRCLE enrollment criteria against similar mitral valve replacement trials globally.

Cortex Search + CKE
3
Always-Current Data

5.7 million trial records from ClinicalTrials.gov via Snowflake Marketplace. Always current. The competitive deck your strategy team builds manually is stale by the time it reaches the CMO.

Snowflake Marketplace CKE
Known Edwards Competitors Found

Tricuspid (vs EVOQUE)

Medtronic Intrepid TTVR, VDyne TRIVITA, Trisol, TRiCares TRICURE, Laplace TTVR

Mitral (vs PASCAL)

Abbott MitraClip (CLASP IID comparator), plus emerging transcatheter mitral replacement devices

TAVR (vs SAPIEN X4)

Medtronic Evolut, JenaValve (now Edwards-owned) for aortic regurgitation indication expansion

Summary & Path Forward

Structured + Unstructured. One Platform.

Governed clinical documents, real-time analytics, competitive intelligence, and AI-powered decision support — all from the platform Edwards already invested in.

For the TMF Manager

3-day audit prep scramble → 30-second query. Real-time document completeness, version tracking, and functional group accountability.

For VP Regulatory

Real-time governance visibility across every trial, every zone, every jurisdiction. One-click audit reports with prioritized action items.

For Clinical Strategy

Competitive intelligence at the speed of a question. 5.7M trial records. Internal + external data in a single query. Always current.

Cortex AI Cortex Agent Production Pilot

Snowflake × Edwards Lifesciences  •  Clinical Document Intelligence for Structural Heart Trials

Prototype Blueprint

From Demo to Production Prototype

The demo proves the AI capabilities. The prototype grounds them in Edwards’ real Veeva Vault data model — making every metric, every search, and every agent answer traceable to your actual eTMF entities.

Layer
What It Contains
Why It Matters
●
Layer 1
Veeva Vault Base Tables

10 tables mirroring Veeva Vault objects: VAULT_STUDY, VAULT_SITE, VAULT_DOCUMENT, VAULT_PERSON, VAULT_EDL, VAULT_EDL_ITEM, VAULT_STUDY_COUNTRY, VAULT_ORGANIZATION, VAULT_STUDY_PERSON, VAULT_QUALITY_ISSUE

Structured metadata lands from Veeva via Direct Data Connector / OpenFlow — the same objects Edwards extracts today via Qlik (documents__v, study__v, site__v, edl__v, etc.)

●
Layer 2
AI-Enriched Tables

PARSED_DOCUMENTS (AI_PARSE_DOCUMENT), DOCUMENT_AI_EXTRACT (AI_EXTRACT), SEARCH_CORPUS (Cortex Search input)

Cortex AI reads the actual PDFs/DOCX from Snowflake Stage, extracts full text + structured entities (endpoints, criteria, amendments) that don’t exist in Veeva metadata

●
Layer 3
Governance & Analytics

TMF_COMPLETENESS_VIEW, TMF_ANALYTICS_VIEW (Semantic View), Cortex Agent (5 tools), Cortex Search, ClinicalTrials.gov CKE

Completeness computed from EDL (expected) vs. actual documents. Semantic View now spans structured Veeva data + AI-enriched content. Agent gains a new study_oversight tool.

Layer 1 is what Veeva gives you. Layer 2 is what Cortex AI adds. Layer 3 is the intelligence that neither can deliver alone.

Layer 1 Detail

Veeva Vault Entities → Snowflake Base Tables

These 10 tables mirror the exact Veeva Vault objects Edwards extracts today. In the prototype, they’re populated with synthetic data matching your 5 active trials. In production, OpenFlow or Direct Data Connector fills them automatically.

Snowflake Table
Veeva Source Object
Key Columns
VAULT_STUDY

study__v

study_id, study_name, device, indication, NCT#, phase, status

VAULT_SITE

site__v

site_id, site_name, study_id, country, activation_date, status

VAULT_DOCUMENT

documents__v

doc_id, study_id, site_id, doc_type, version, lifecycle_state, is_deleted

VAULT_EDL

edl__v

edl_id, study_id, tmf_zone, tmf_section — the “expected” TMF template

VAULT_EDL_ITEM

edl_item__v

edl_item_id, edl_id, expected_doc_type, site_level, required, status

VAULT_PERSON

person__sys

person_id, name, email, organization_id

VAULT_STUDY_PERSON

study_person__clin

study_id, person_id, site_id, role (PI, CRA, Monitor)

VAULT_STUDY_COUNTRY

study_country__v

study_id, country, enrollment_status, target_enrollment

VAULT_ORGANIZATION

organization__v

org_id, org_name, org_type (Sponsor, CRO, Site)

VAULT_QUALITY_ISSUE

quality_issue__v

issue_id, study_id, site_id, doc_id, severity, status

These are the same 13 objects Edwards currently extracts from Veeva Vault via Qlik — now landing directly in Snowflake.

Prototype Value

What This Enables

Grounding the demo in Veeva Vault entities unlocks capabilities that matter to Edwards today — not as future roadmap, but as prototype-ready outcomes.

✔ Real TMF Completeness

Compare EDL (expected documents) vs. actual documents filed. No more fabricated completeness — the score is computed from the same TMF template Edwards uses in Veeva Vault.

Why it matters: Inspection-grade completeness, not demo-grade estimates.

✔ ISF Reconciliation

Cross-reference site-level expected docs vs. filed docs per site. This is Edwards’ Priority 2 use case from the Veeva Vault discovery.

Why it matters: ISF reconciliation is the #1 inspection finding across the industry.

✔ Expired Document Monitoring

With EDL expected dates + document lifecycle states from Veeva, compute exactly which documents are past due — not approximated from upload timestamps.

Why it matters: Edwards’ Priority 1 business need. Currently done manually.

✔ Study Oversight & Site Intelligence

Enrollment by country, site activation status, PI assignments, quality issue tracking — all queryable through the AI Agent. “Which sites for TRISCEND II haven’t activated yet?”

Why it matters: Operational visibility that currently requires manual Veeva Vault exports.

The demo showed what’s possible. The prototype makes it real — grounded in Edwards’ actual Veeva Vault entities, with the same objects your teams extract today.
